mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand contraction and recessionary pressures, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during post-2009 recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary overheating, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or interaction term IC is negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500,000 units indicate a collapsing construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 50% of months where HOUST drops below 500.0, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low federal funds rates and declining unemployment creates an optimal liquidity and growth environment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains flat, or joint signal IC turns negative during periods of stagflationary pressure.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product confirm broad-based economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5%, or the signal shows negative IC during late-cycle phases.
